title: Coronopus lepidioides
intro: Coronopus lepidioides, also known as Sea Rocket, is a perennial, succulent herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as colds, coughs, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Coronopus lepidioides include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into skincare products and poultices to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Coronopus lepidioides is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. This species belongs to the genus Coronopus, a group of small, annual or perennial plants. Coronopus lepidioides is also classified as a member of the order Brassicales, which comprises flowering plants with four-petaled flowers. The species is further grouped as part of the superorder Rosanae, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. Its classification is also supported by molecular studies, which confirm its relationships within the Brassicaceae family.
uses: Coronopus lepidioides, commonly known as sea purslane or sea pursley, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
